trade.summary: Solid bilateral trade. Thailand exports electronics, machinery, rubber, fresh produce, and seafood to Netherlands (EU gateway); Netherlands exports chemicals, agricultural products, machinery, and high-tech equipment. Rotterdam serves as entry point for Thai goods into Europe. Dutch agricultural companies (Philips Horticulture, Priva) are active in Thailand's modern agriculture sector.

diplomatic.summary: Good bilateral relations under the EU-Thailand framework. Netherlands occasionally raised concerns about Thai human rights under military government (2014-2023). Relations normalized following Thai return to electoral politics. Netherlands is an important market and partner for Thailand's agricultural and electronics exports.

military.summary: No significant bilateral military relationship. Netherlands has sold limited naval equipment to Thailand. Both countries have interacted through multilateral security dialogues.

financial.summary: Netherlands is a significant investor in Thailand, with Shell, Unilever, and ING having Thai operations. Dutch pension funds hold Thai equities. Thai investment in the Netherlands is limited.

energy.summary: Netherlands exports refined petroleum to Thailand. Shell has downstream (retail fuel) operations in Thailand. No significant natural gas bilateral trade.

scientific.summary: Active cooperation in agricultural research, water management, and horticulture technology. Wageningen University has research partnerships with Thai agricultural institutions. Dutch horticulture innovations are widely applied in Thailand.
